tokamaker-jax educational browser companion

MHD Solver Explorer

A self-contained interface for learning the pieces of tokamaker-jax: Grad-Shafranov equilibria, free-boundary coil responses, nonlinear profiles, triangular FEM assembly, validation gates, TOML inputs, and CLI workflows.

J x B = grad(p) -> Delta* psi = -mu0 R^2 p'(psi) - F F'(psi)
solver.py + profiles.py

Equilibrium Inputs

fixed boundary

Residual
psi range
Magnetic axis
Plasma shaping

Flux Surfaces

step 90

Equation

axisymmetric MHD
Delta* psi = -mu0 R^2 p'(psi) - F(psi) F'(psi)

The browser solve is reduced and immediate; exported TOML runs through the Python/JAX path for reproducible calculations.

What to try

Raise pressure to deepen the flux well, increase shaping current to move the axis, then use triangularity to make the outboard side D-shaped.

Run in tokamaker-jax

TOML

Coil Inputs

free boundary

Peak psi
Peak field
Linearity error
Response points

Coil Green Response

psi and B

Midplane Cut

per amp response

Implemented Path

free_boundary.py
  • circular_loop_elliptic_flux and gradients for differentiable circular-filament response.
  • coil_response_matrix and evaluate_coil_green_response for coil-to-point coupling.
  • Reduced log Green functions remain useful for small validation fixtures and classroom derivations.

Validation Prompt

linearity + gradients

Move the coil close to the plasma edge and increase the core radius. The response smooths near the filament while the far-field symmetry remains visible.

tokamaker-jax verify --gate circular-loop --subdivisions 4 8 16

Profile Inputs

Picard iteration

Residual final
Update final
Source peak
Pressure grad

Model

fem_equilibrium.py
p'(psi_n) = scale * (1 - psi_n)^(alpha gamma)

The full implementation assembles a flux-dependent P1 triangular-FEM load vector and iterates the fixed-boundary nonlinear profile solve.

-div((1/R) grad psi) = 0.5 F F' / R + mu0 R p'

Profile TOML

educational export

Mesh Inputs

P1 triangles

Nodes
Cells
Boundary nodes
Min area

Triangular Mesh and Regions

geometry.py + mesh.py

Element Matrix

assembly.py

Weak Form

differentiable assembly
A_ij = integral_Omega (1/R) grad phi_i . grad phi_j dR dZ

Fixed topology and JAX arrays keep element maps, assembly kernels, and solves transformable for sensitivity studies.

M_ij = integral_Omega phi_i phi_j dR dZ

Region Types

sample geometry
PlasmaD-shaped polygon
Wallannular shell
PF coilsrectangles
Vacuumbackground cells

Gate Inputs

reproducibility

Observed rate
Target
Primary error
Artifact

Validation Curve

log-log convergence

IO Contracts

config + eqdsk
  • load_config parses Python 3.10+ TOML with tomli fallback.
  • parse_eqdsk and diagnose_eqdsk expose reconstruction metadata for comparison fixtures.
  • case_manifest records runnable examples and validation workflows.

Selected Gate

details

Workflow Picker

CLI default

Python3.10+
Default commandGUI
Config formatTOML
Core array pathJAX

Feature Map

package surfaces

Quick Start

copy and run
git clone git@github.com:rogeriojorge/tokamaker_jax.git cd tokamaker_jax python -m pip install -e . tokamaker-jax

With no TOML path, the command opens the GUI. With a TOML path, it runs a reproducible solve and can write arrays, plots, or validation reports.

Example TOML

fixed boundary
